title: crepidomanes grande
intro: Crepidomanes grande, also known as the giant fork-leaved spleenwort, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is native to tropical forests in the Philippines and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, Crepidomanes grande is typically dried and processed into teas, infusions, or extracts that can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
